fields.label: Event
fields.rationale: Load-bearing. Use New to alert approvers BEFORE they act (their submit is what fires the event, so Submit on the approver would email them about their own click). Use Submit for post-approval downstream notifications. Value Change fires when value_changed (specific field) mutates. Days After / Days Before require date_changed AND fire via the daily scheduler - the date_changed field must be a Date / Datetime field on the DocType. Minutes After / Minutes Before require datetime_changed AND minutes_offset >= 10 (the minimum is enforced because the scheduler tick is coarser than a few minutes). Method fires when a specific method (named in the method field) is called on the document.

fields.label: Channel
fields.rationale: Email is the canonical channel. System Notification is free (no SMTP / gateway) but only visible inside Desk. Slack requires slack_webhook_url. SMS requires an SMS gateway configured in System Settings. set_only_once - cannot swap channels on a saved Notification.

fields.label: Date field (for Days After / Days Before)
fields.rationale: Required when event is Days After or Days Before. Names a Date / Datetime field on the target DocType. Empty for other events.

fields.label: Days offset
fields.rationale: Number of days before (for Days Before) or after (for Days After) the date_changed value. Default 0 (same day).

fields.label: Field to watch (for Value Change)
fields.rationale: Required when event is Value Change. Names the field whose mutation triggers the notification. Empty for other events.

fields.label: Method name (for event='Method')
fields.rationale: Required when event=Method. Names the method on the document that, when called, triggers the notification. Empty for other events.

fields.label: Recipients (list)
fields.rationale: At least one recipient row is required UNLESS send_to_all_assignees=1 or channel=Slack (Slack uses slack_webhook_url instead). Each row is either `{receiver_by_document_field: 'expense_approver'}` (dynamic: Link field on the target resolves to a User), `{receiver_by_role: 'Accounts Manager'}` (static: every user with that role), or `{email: 'ops@example.com'}` (fully static). Prefer dynamic receiver_by_document_field for per-document routing.

fields.label: Subject (Jinja)
fields.rationale: Jinja-rendered over the target document. Reference fields via {{ doc.fieldname }}. validate_template checks syntax at save time - a malformed Jinja subject is rejected. Empty defaults to a generic system subject for Email.

fields.label: Message body (Jinja HTML)
fields.rationale: Jinja-rendered body. validate_template checks syntax at save. Keep short and actionable. {{ frappe.utils.get_url() }} for stable site URLs.

fields.label: Condition type
fields.rationale: Python uses the `condition` field (a Python expression evaluated with frappe.safe_eval on a dummy doc at save and on the real doc at fire time). Filters uses the `filters` field (JSON filter-triple list evaluated via evaluate_filters). Switching condition_type automatically clears the OTHER field on save.

fields.label: Condition (Python expression)
fields.rationale: Optional Python expression used when condition_type=Python. Example: `doc.amount > 10000`. Evaluated with frappe.safe_eval (no imports, limited globals). False / exception suppresses the notification silently. Empty means fire on every matching event.

fields.label: Enabled
fields.rationale: Default enabled. Flip off for staging. is_standard=1 + enabled=1 outside developer_mode is rejected.

fields.label: Also send System Notification
fields.rationale: When 1, creates a Notification Log entry even if channel is Email / Slack / SMS. Useful when you want both an email AND a Desk notification bell alert.
